title: senecio werneriifolius var. alpinus
intro: Senecio werneriifolius var. alpinus, also known as alpine wormwood or alpine ragwort, is a lesser-known variety of the Senecio werneriifolius plant, which is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been shown to possess medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Senecio werneriifolius var. alpinus include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, but it is essential to note that excessive consumption or improper preparation can be toxic due to its alkaloid content.
